CONSIDERABLE STRUCTURE AND TREE DAMAGE IN WILD ROSE AREA SOME ROADS IMPASSABLE NORTH AND EAST PART OF COUNTY DUE TO TREE DAMAGE.
THE TRINITY LUTHERAN CHURCH HAS SUSTAINED HEAVY DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE CITY.

TORNADO WAS IMBEDDED IN A LARGE AREA OF STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS. A CHURCH SUSTAINED HEAVY DAMAGE...EF2...WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OVER 110 MPH. IN ADDITION...HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED...CAUSING DAMAGE TO HOMES IN THE NEW LONDON AREA. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ESE TOWARD HORTONVILLE. IN HORTONVILLE...HUNDREDS OF HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN CITY.
MANY TREES DOWN AND CAMPERS FLIPPED OVER AT THE HUCKLEBERRY CAMPGROUND. POSSIBLE INJURIES.
NO POWER IN CITY. MANY TREES SNAPPED.
MANY LARGE TREES SNAPPED...POWERLINES DOWN. NO POWER IN CITY.
A TORNADO FORMED NEAR MACKVILLE AND MOVED SOUTHEAST FOR APPROX 4.8 MILES...LIFTING OFF ABOUT 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF KAUKAUNA. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 90 TO 100 MPH.

THE SAME STORM THAT PRODUCED THE TORNADO DAMAGE IN NEW LONDON AND HORTONVILLE CAUSED HEAVY DAMAGE ON THE NORTH SIDE OF APPLETON. NUMEROUS HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES. SEVERAL LARGE WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED NEAR THE NORTHLAND MALL. BUILDINGS IN THE AREA SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. PRELIMINARY RATING IS EF1 WITH WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH. THIS STORM CONTINUED ESE INTO FAR SW BROWN COUNTY AND N CALUMET COUNTY.

A TORNADO FORMED NORTHEAST OF MACKVILLE AND MOVED EAST FOR ABOUT 30 MILES...DIMINISHING A FEW MILES EAST OF MARIBEL /MANITOWOC COUNTY/. THE TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED 500 TO 1000 TREES ALONG THE PATH. SWIRL MARKS WERE SEEN IN FLATTENED CORN FIELDS ALONG THE PATH. NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. SEVERAL BARNS SUSTAINED MODERATE TO HEAVY DAMAGE. SEVERAL HOMES AND BUSINESSES SUSTAINED DAMAGE TO ROOFS OR SIDING. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH.

A TORNADO WAS IMBEDDED IN A LARGE AREA OF STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS. TORNADIC DAMAGE OCCURRED IN FAR SOUTHERN BROWN COUNTY AND MOVED ACROSS THE COUNTY LINE INTO CALUMET COUNTY NEAR HWY 57 AND COUNTY RD KK. AT LEAST TWO BARNS SUSTAINED HEAVY DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. TWO METAL TRUSS TOWERS WERE BLOWN DOWN IN CALUMET COUNTY. PRELIMINARY RATING FOR THIS TORNADO IS EF1 WITH WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH.
HWY 57 CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS AT THE BROWN/CALUMET LINE DUE TO A UTILITY EMERGENCY.
SEMI-TRAILER OVERTURNED ON INTERSTATE 43 NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD Z. REPORTED BY FOX 11.
A BRIEF TORNADO HIT NEAR THE BROWN/KEWAUNEE COUNTY LINE CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES AND TWO BARNS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 3 MILES. IT WAS RATED EF1 WITH WINDS ESTIMATED 85 TO 90 MPH.
